source_jobs.title: Regional Director
source_jobs.description:
THE OPPORTUNITY

ModernFi is seeking a Regional Director to own the client relationships across the full institution journey, from activation through long-term growth. You will play a crucial role in ensuring client satisfaction, driving product adoption, and fostering long-term relationships with our clients. As the relationship owner for your portfolio, you will ensure clients understand and realize the full value of our platform, surfacing opportunities tied to their growth goals and delivering client feedback to our product teams.

Your work will directly strengthen how financial institutions across the county operate, improving the financial health of millions of Americans. This role offers the opportunity to drive revenue, deepen client success, influence product development, and shape ModernFi's strategic direction during a period of rapid growth.

At ModernFi, your work will directly fuel our vision and mission to serve as systemically important financial infrastructure that drives the American economy and to empower our partner financial institutions to grow and better serve their communities.




THE ROLE


RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Lead the institution activation process, ensuring a smooth transition from sales to active usage of ModernFi's platform

 - Be a strategic partner to our clients, ensuring they understand and can effectively utilize all platform features

 - Oversee joint marketing activations with clients, coordinating between internal teams (Institution Success, Marketing, Sales) and client stakeholders

 - Provide ongoing support to clients, addressing inquiries and resolving issues promptly

 - Regularly check in on client usage, identify patterns, and proactively suggest ways to increase platform utilization

 - Develop and maintain strong, long-lasting client relationships, serving as the primary point of contact

 - Work closely with founding team and the Institution Success team to develop strategies for increasing client engagement and usage

 - Identify and communicate upsell opportunities based on client's strategic growth goals

 - Gather and communicate client feedback on the product to drive platform improvements


ABOUT YOU

 - Energized by collaborating with clients and experience in a revenue-generating function

 - 5+ years of experience in relationship management, account management or similar roles

 - Excellent communication and interpersonal skills for client-facing and internal interactions

 - Energized by meeting with key partners in person (and willingness to travel accordingly)

 - Proven ability to manage multiple clients and work streams simultaneously

 - Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail

 - High level of organization and professionalism, and comfort with data-driven marketing practices

 - Ability to handle nebulous problems and shifting priorities with flexibility, patience, and skill

 - Excited by the prospect of life- and career-changing work in a startup environment

 - High EQ, trustworthy, and calm under pressure

 - Strong understanding of financial systems, markets, technology, and banking




ABOUT US

The caliber and character of the ModernFi team set us apart.

We value competence and drive over experience, and look for compassionate people with diverse backgrounds. We appreciate technical ability, dedication, and humble confidence. Above all, we value respect — for our colleagues, our clients, our partners, and ourselves.

Our team pushes each other to be better every day, supports each other when things go wrong, and grows together on this journey. Because we are a close-knit team, we are selective and considerate with every hire.

The people at ModernFi could be doing anything in the world, yet they choose to be here.




BENEFITS

 - Top-of-market comp, equity from day one

 - Health, dental, vision. 100% employee covered

 - 401(k) with a broad fund selection

 - Unlimited PTO

 - Fully paid parental leave

 - $100 monthly wellness stipend

source_jobs.title: Strategic Client Solutions
source_jobs.description:
THE OPPORTUNITY

ModernFi is seeking an individual for the role of Strategic Client Solutions to serve as a critical subject matter expert (SME) on our market, product, and operational capabilities throughout the entire client journey. In this essential role, you will work alongside Account Executives during the pre-sale process and collaborate with Regional Directors post-sale to ensure clients realize maximum value from our platform. As the technical knowledge cornerstone of our deal teams, you will drive client success by providing expert guidance, solving complex challenges, and ensuring our solutions deliver measurable business outcomes.

Your expertise will be instrumental in helping clients understand, implement, and optimize ModernFi's platform for their specific needs. This position offers the opportunity to directly drive revenue, ensure continued client success, influence product development, and shape ModernFi's strategic direction during a period of rapid growth.

At ModernFi, your work will directly fuel our vision and mission to serve as systemically important financial infrastructure that drives the American economy and to empower our partner financial institutions to grow and better serve their communities.




THE ROLE


RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Serve as the definitive subject matter expert on ModernFi's market positioning, product capabilities, and operational processes across all client interactions.

 - Partner with Account Executives during the pre-sale process to articulate technical value propositions, address complex questions, and ensure solutions align with client needs.

 - Work closely with Regional Directors post-sale to ensure clients achieve their strategic objectives and realize full value from our platform.

 - Drive technical implementation success by ensuring proper configuration and integration with client systems.

 - Guide clients through technical challenges, providing expert consultation on how to maximize platform benefits for their specific use cases.

 - Develop and deliver specialized knowledge transfer sessions that empower clients to leverage our platform's full capabilities.

 - Create and maintain enablement resources, alongside marketing managers and other teammates, that showcase platform value and accelerate client success.

 - Identify opportunities to expand platform usage within client organizations based on deep understanding of their needs and our capabilities.

 - Provide valuable product and market feedback to internal teams based on client interactions and industry knowledge.

 - Serve as the escalation point for complex client questions that require deep product and operational expertise.

 - Participate in deal team meetings as the technical authority to support client acquisition, retention, and expansion efforts.

 - Track and analyze client usage patterns to identify opportunities for enhancing value realization.


ABOUT YOU

 - Deep understanding of financial technology markets, products, and operational considerations.

 - Exceptional ability to translate complex technical concepts into clear value propositions for diverse audiences.

 - Proven track record of helping clients achieve tangible business outcomes through technology solutions.

 - Strong consultative skills with the ability to understand client needs and recommend appropriate solutions.

 - Experience functioning as a trusted advisor throughout the entire client lifecycle.

 - Excellent presentation and communication skills, with the ability to engage effectively in both technical and business discussions.

 - Strategic mindset that can connect client challenges to platform capabilities and identify value creation opportunities.

 - Team-oriented approach with experience working collaboratively on deal teams alongside sales and customer success personnel.

 - Ability to balance technical expertise with business acumen in client-facing situations.

 - Strong problem-solving abilities with a solutions-or

source_jobs.title: Growth Manager
source_jobs.description:
THE OPPORTUNITY

ModernFi is seeking a Growth Manager to own and drive our growth function during a critical expansion phase. This is an operator role with high autonomy, high output, and built for someone who executes with precision. You will develop and execute strategies that drive brand awareness, demand generation, and revenue enablement by leveraging AI tooling, cross-functional collaboration with Sales, Post-Sales, and Product, and external agencies and contractors. You will own strategy, content creation, digital channels, and analytics while building the operational foundation that scales with us. This role is for a builder who wants to be exceptional at what modern growth execution looks like and who sees AI as a core part of how great work gets done.

At ModernFi, your work will directly fuel our vision and mission to serve as systemically important financial infrastructure that drives the American economy and to empower our partner financial institutions to grow and better serve their communities.




THE ROLE


RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Create compelling content across multiple formats (website copy, blog posts, emails, social media, sales collateral, case studies, etc.), leveraging AI tools to increase output quality and velocity.

 - Build and manage our digital presence across website, social media, and email channels.

 - Design and produce collateral (alongside external agencies and contractors as needed) including presentations, one-pagers, and newsletters that clearly communicate our value proposition to financial institutions.

 - Coordinate and execute growth programs including webinars, executive dinners, industry events, and digital campaigns.

 - Partner closely with Sales, Post-Sales, and Product to develop materials that support the deal cycle, accelerate conversion, and reinforce customer value.

 - Establish metrics to measure growth effectiveness and continuously optimize based on data.

 - Develop and execute ModernFi's growth strategy, including positioning, messaging, and channel strategy.

 - Stay current on fintech industry trends and competitive landscape to inform strategy.

 - Manage relationships with external agencies and contractors as needed.
   
   


ABOUT YOU

 - 2+ years of growth, content, or demand generation experience - preferably in fintech, B2B SaaS, or financial services - with demonstrated professional growth across your career.

 - Strong content creation skills with the ability to translate complex technical concepts into compelling messaging.

 - Fluent with AI tools and actively uses them to work faster and better; you're not waiting for permission to experiment.

 - Experience with digital channels and automation tools.

 - Data-driven approach with experience using analytics to measure and improve programs.

 - Exceptional communication skills, both written and verbal.

 - Comfortable operating cross-functionally - you know how to work with Sales, Post-Sales, and Product without needing a playbook handed to you.

 - Exceptional attention to detail with a high-quality bar for written and visual work.

 - Creative eye for design and content, with experience producing polished assets.

 - Proactive self-starter who takes initiative to learn new tools independently.

 - Thrives in ambitious, high-velocity environments and brings the same energy and commitment to their professional work that high-performers bring to any demanding pursuit.

 - Track record of meaningful tenure and ownership within previous roles - comfortable being the one who builds, writes, and ships.




ABOUT US

The caliber and character of the ModernFi team set us apart.

We value competence and drive over experience, and look for compassionate people with diverse backgrounds. We appreciate technical ability, dedication, and humble confidence. Above all, we value respect — for our colleagues, our clients, our partners, and ourselves.

Our team pushes each other to be better every day, supports each other w

source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Engineer
source_jobs.description:
THE OPPORTUNITY

ModernFi is seeking a Forward Deployed Engineer to lead the technical onboarding of financial institutions onto our platform throughout each client's implementation journey. In this essential role, you will sit on the Engineering team and work across functions to ensure every institution experiences a seamless and reliable path to going live. As the technical cornerstone of each client implementation, you will drive client success by setting up infrastructure, configuring integrations, communicating directly with partners, and building the tooling that makes the process better for everyone involved.

Your work will be instrumental in helping banks and credit unions integrate, configure, and adopt ModernFi for their specific needs, translating requirements between technical and non-technical stakeholders along the way. This role offers the opportunity to lead the projects that unlock revenue, drive client success, inform how we build the product, and shape how ModernFi scales onboarding during a period of rapid growth.

At ModernFi, your work will directly fuel our vision and mission to serve as systemically important financial infrastructure that drives the American economy and to empower our partner financial institutions to grow and better serve their communities.




THE ROLE


RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Lead technical onboarding of new institutions to the ModernFi platform — from account setup to configuration, testing, and go-live

 - Manage infrastructure setup for clients, including provisioning SFTP servers and keypairs with Pulumi

 - Collaborate with bank IT teams to configure SSO in Auth0 based on their identity providers

 - Coordinate onboarding for third-party digital banking integrations like Q2, serving as the glue between ModernFi, the client, and the partner

 - Own and improve our public API documentation — making it easier for clients to integrate and showcasing our engineering-first approach

 - Build tooling, dashboards, or internal features (using Python, Retool, etc.) that make your work more efficient and scalable

 - Optimize our self-service onboarding tool by partnering with our CS team to intake client feedback and provide product suggestions or own immediate fixes

 - Translate between technical and non-technical audiences with empathy and clarity


ABOUT YOU

 - Engineering background, with a CS degree or equivalent experience

 - Comfortable working with code and infrastructure — you may not write production services day to day, but you can spin up an SFTP server, tweak a Python script, or make a SQL query

 - Excellent communicator who can guide clients through technical setup and translate their needs to the engineering team

 - Excited by the idea of improving the customer experience through a mix of software, process, and grit

 - Collaborative and fun to work with — you bring positive energy to the team

 - Experience with tools like AWS, Auth0, Pulumi, Python, PostgreSQL, or Retool is a plus but not required
   


ABOUT US

The caliber and character of the ModernFi team set us apart.

We value competence and drive over experience, and look for compassionate people with diverse backgrounds. We appreciate technical ability, dedication, and humble confidence. Above all, we value respect — for our colleagues, our clients, our partners, and ourselves.

Our team pushes each other to be better every day, supports each other when things go wrong, and grows together on this journey. Because we are a close-knit team, we are selective and considerate with every hire.

The people at ModernFi could be doing anything in the world, yet they choose to be here.




BENEFITS

 - Top-of-market comp, equity from day one

 - Health, dental, vision. 100% employee covered

 - 401(k) with a broad fund selection

 - Unlimited PTO

 - Fully paid parental leave

 - $100 monthly wellness stipend

 - Dinner in the office

 - Relocation to New York

 - Uncapped equipment

 - Quarterly team outings

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
THE OPPORTUNITY

ModernFi is seeking software engineers to build the foundation of the platform that powers banks and credit unions nationwide. From day one, you will have significant ownership and the opportunity to shape the direction of our platform, our engineering culture, and the broader trajectory of the company. As a member of a talented and collaborative team, you will design and build the systems at the core of how ModernFi operates.

Your work will be instrumental in building the products that help banks and credit unions better serve their communities, improving financial health nationwide. This role offers the opportunity to build impactful solutions with real autonomy, work on mission-critical systems, grow your technical expertise, and influence how the team builds as we scale during a period of rapid growth.

We are hiring across levels (mid-level, Senior, and Staff), and your level will be determined during the interview process based on your technical performance.

At ModernFi, your work will directly fuel our vision and mission to serve as systemically important financial infrastructure that drives the American economy and to empower our partner financial institutions to grow and better serve their communities.




THE ROLE


RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Design and build scalable, maintainable, and correct backend services to support ModernFi’s core products and bank integrations.

 - Lead or contribute to technical initiatives, with opportunities to define architecture, lead projects, and mentor teammates.

 - Partner closely with Product to define requirements, translate business needs into technical specs, and ship elegant, impactful features.

 - Uphold and evolve engineering best practices, including code quality, testing, CI/CD pipelines, and documentation.

 - Participate in design discussions, conduct code reviews, and contribute to technical decisions that scale with our business.

 - Tackle challenging technical problems with innovative and pragmatic solutions.

 - Help cultivate an inclusive, high-performing engineering culture grounded in trust, ownership, and collaboration.


TECH STACK

 - Python (FastAPI, SQLAlchemy), Typescript

 - Retool (React, Vite, Tailwind)

 - PostgreSQL Database

 - AWS / Pulumi IaC

 - Tools: Auth0, Datadog, Fern


ABOUT YOU

 - Proven ability to independently design, develop, and maintain backend systems.

 - Strong understanding of software engineering principles, with tangible experience writing high-quality, production-ready code.

 - Experience leading or contributing significantly to complex engineering projects (for more senior candidates).

 - Excellent communication skills with the ability to explain technical concepts clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences.

 - Comfortable operating in a fast-paced, dynamic environment with shifting priorities.

 - High level of empathy, integrity, and calmness under pressure.

 - 4+ years of software engineering experience (flexible depending on level), ideally with Python, AWS, or similar modern technologies.

 - Interest in financial systems and the opportunity to work on meaningful problems with real-world impact

 - You’re a thoughtful IC who thrives on shipping great software, improving team practices, and supporting those around you.




ABOUT US

The caliber and character of the ModernFi team set us apart.

We value competence and drive over experience, and look for compassionate people with diverse backgrounds. We appreciate technical ability, dedication, and humble confidence. Above all, we value respect — for our colleagues, our clients, our partners, and ourselves.

Our team pushes each other to be better every day, supports each other when things go wrong, and grows together on this journey. Because we are a close-knit team, we are selective and considerate with every hire.

The people at ModernFi could be doing anything in the world, yet they choose to be here.




BENEFITS

 - Top-of-market comp, equity

source_jobs.title: Business Development Representative
source_jobs.description:
THE OPPORTUNITY

As a Business Development Representative at ModernFi, you will be on the front lines of our growth engine. You’ll focus on driving new opportunities by prospecting, cold calling, and engaging decision-makers across the banking and credit union industry. Your role will be essential in building the top of the sales funnel, qualifying opportunities, and partnering with Account Executives to move deals forward.

This is a chance to gain impact and responsibility from day one, playing a critical role in bringing new institutions onto the ModernFi platform. You’ll develop outreach strategies, sharpen your sales skills, and learn directly from a seasoned team of fintech sales leaders.

At ModernFi, your work will directly fuel our vision and mission to serve as systemically important financial infrastructure that drives the American economy and to empower our partner financial institutions to grow and better serve their communities.



THE ROLE


RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Proactively generate top-of-funnel opportunities through cold calling, emailing, LinkedIn outreach, and other prospecting methods

 - Research and identify target banks and credit unions that align with ModernFi’s solutions

 - Qualify inbound and outbound leads, ensuring high-quality opportunities are passed to Account Executives

 - Collaborate with Account Executives to support the sales cycle and drive revenue growth

 - Maintain clean and accurate records in CRM systems, tracking pipeline activity and outreach metrics

 - Experiment with messaging and outreach strategies to maximize engagement with prospective clients

 - Consistently meet or exceed weekly and monthly activity metrics for qualified opportunities created and revenue generate


ABOUT YOU

 - 1–3 years of experience in business development, inside sales, or sales development (preferably in fintech, SaaS, or financial services)

 - Comfortable making cold calls and engaging senior executives in financial institutions

 - Strong organizational skills and ability to manage multiple outreach sequences simultaneously

 - High resilience and persistence in building relationships and overcoming objections

 - Excellent verbal and written communication skills; able to craft compelling outreach messages

 - Eager to learn, receptive to feedback, and excited to grow into a future sales or partnerships role

 - Entrepreneurial spirit — thrives in a fast-paced, high-growth environment with a high degree of autonomy
   
   


ABOUT US

The caliber and character of the ModernFi team set us apart.

We value competence and drive over experience, and look for compassionate people with diverse backgrounds. We appreciate technical ability, dedication, and humble confidence. Above all, we value respect — for our colleagues, our clients, our partners, and ourselves.

Our team pushes each other to be better every day, supports each other when things go wrong, and grows together on this journey. Because we are a close-knit team, we are selective and considerate with every hire.

The people at ModernFi could be doing anything in the world, yet they choose to be here.




BENEFITS

 - Top-of-market comp, equity from day one

 - Health, dental, vision. 100% employee covered

 - 401(k) with a broad fund selection

 - Unlimited PTO

 - Fully paid parental leave

 - $100 monthly wellness stipend

 - Dinner in the office

 - Relocation to New York

 - Uncapped equipment

 - Quarterly team outings
   
   


THE PROCESS

If you’d like to learn more, please email us at careers@modernfi.com. Our process is focused on cultural and personality fit. We are excited to get to know you, learn about what motivates you, and ensure that we can accelerate your personal and professional growth.

source_jobs.title: Engineering Manager
source_jobs.description:
THE OPPORTUNITY

ModernFi is seeking an Engineering Manager to build and scale the platform that powers banks and credit unions nationwide. In this pivotal role, you will work directly with our leadership team to set engineering direction and ensure the team delivers with both speed and rigor. As the leader of a world-class engineering team, you will guide execution, elevate technical standards, and empower engineers to do the best work of their careers.

Your leadership will be instrumental in building the technology that helps financial institutions operate, strengthening the financial health of millions of Americans. This role offers the opportunity to shape our engineering culture and practices from the ground up, scale the systems our platform depends on, grow a deeply skilled team, and influence ModernFi's broader trajectory during a period of rapid growth.

At ModernFi, your work will directly fuel our vision and mission to serve as systemically important financial infrastructure that drives the American economy and to empower our partner financial institutions to grow and better serve their communities.




THE ROLE


RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Lead, mentor, and develop a high-performing engineering team, fostering growth, accountability, and technical excellence

 - Drive end-to-end delivery of high-impact engineering initiatives - from concept to architecture, implementation, testing, and launch

 - Partner closely with Product, business stakeholders, and leadership to define scope, align on priorities, and ensure ModernFi ships high-quality features quickly and reliably

 - Establish and continuously improve engineering best practices, tools, and processes for a rapidly scaling organization

 - Own all the code your team produces. Review all code and not be afraid to hop into it and help push things over the line. At this stage, we are all ICs regardless of title and do whatever it takes to move towards our goals.

 - Support hiring, onboarding, and leveling efforts to help build a diverse, exceptional engineering team

 - Ensure strong execution through clear goal-setting, effective sprint planning, and proactive risk mitigation

 - Maintain a high technical bar by participating in architecture discussions, reviewing code, and guiding engineers in solving complex technical problems

 - Collaborate cross-functionally to translate client feedback and business needs into scalable technical solutions

 - Promote a culture of extreme ownership, thoughtful iteration, and high accountability within the engineering organization

 - Contribute to long-term technical strategy, system design, and infrastructure decisions as we scale to serve increasingly sophisticated institutions


TECH STACK

 - Python (FastAPI, SQLAlchemy), Typescript

 - Retool (React, Vite, Tailwind)

 - PostgreSQL Database

 - AWS / Pulumi IaC

 - Tools: Auth0, Datadog, Fern


ABOUT YOU

 - 6+ years of professional software engineering experience, including 2+ years in an engineering leadership or tech lead role

 - Strong technical foundation - capable of diving deep into architecture, system design, and debugging across the stack

 - Experience scaling an Engineering org. We are one team today, but just at the boundary, and will soon need to split into multiple squads.

 - Proven ability to lead teams through ambiguity and guide engineers toward clear outcomes

 - Comfortable in a fast-moving startup environment with evolving priorities

 - Strong communicator who can articulate technical concepts clearly across engineering, product, and leadership

 - High EQ, trustworthy, calm under pressure, and committed to developing others

 - Experience hiring, coaching, and conducting performance management in a high-expectation environment

 - Bias for action, strong ownership mentality, and passion for building mission-critical systems

 - Interest in (or experience with) fintech, banking, financial systems, or highly regulated environments is a plus

 - Energized

source_jobs.title: Platform Engineer
source_jobs.description:
THE OPPORTUNITY

ModernFi is seeking a Platform Engineer to build the foundation that our platform runs on, alongside a talented and collaborative team. This is a founding Platform Engineering role, and from day one you will work on critical systems with meaningful ownership and a direct hand in shaping the direction of our products and infrastructure. As the technical owner of the platform, you will define how we operate our infrastructure, release software, and approach security across the company.

Your work will be instrumental in building the products that help banks and credit unions operate, improving financial health nationwide. This role offers the opportunity to deliver impactful solutions with real autonomy, work on mission-critical systems, grow your technical expertise, and influence ModernFi's broader trajectory as we grow from serving hundreds of institutions to thousands.

At ModernFi, your work will directly fuel our vision and mission to serve as systemically important financial infrastructure that drives the American economy and to empower our partner financial institutions to grow and better serve their communities.




THE ROLE


RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own and evolve the reliability posture of the ModernFi network (observability, incident response, SLOs, and on-call practices) so we can keep shipping fast without ever compromising correctness.

 - Lead the buildout of a more advanced release pipeline with merge queues, feature-flag tooling, and per-domain release gates for our highest-stakes services across multiple environments.

 - Take ownership of our cloud platform, Pulumi modules, AWS account structure, identity, networking, and the tooling product engineers build on, and make the right call between extending what we have today and replacing it for what's next.

 - Drive our security and compliance posture: Vanta remediation, SOC 1/2 readiness, vulnerability scanning, secrets and key management, and hardening across the stack — partnering closely with our CCO, external advisors, and the rest of engineering.

 - Upgrade how ModernFi does on-call: the tooling around it, the runbooks behind it, the rotations that staff it, and the postmortems that close it out while participating in the infrastructure on-call rotation yourself.

 - Partner closely with our product engineering pods to understand where the platform creates drag, and ship the abstractions that let them move faster and more safely.

 - Cultivate a culture of operational excellence through code reviews, design discussions, mentorship, and the steady raising of our collective bar for what production-grade looks like.


TECH STACK

 - AWS / Pulumi IaC

 - PostgreSQL (RDS), ECS, Docker

 - Temporal

 - Python (FastAPI, SQLAlchemy)

 - Datadog, PagerDuty, GitHub Actions

 - Auth0, Vanta, Fern


ABOUT YOU

 - 5+ years of software engineering experience, with significant time spent on infrastructure, platform, or reliability-focused work in a production environment.

 - Deep fluency with cloud infrastructure (ideally AWS), infrastructure-as-code (Pulumi, Terraform, or similar), and modern CI/CD pipelines.

 - A security mindset: you treat security as a first-class concern in everything you build, even if you don't come from a dedicated security background. Familiarity with frameworks such as SOC 2, compliance tooling such as Vanta, and concepts such as secrets management, key rotation, and least-privilege access is a plus.

 - Hands-on experience with a modern observability stack (Datadog, Prometheus + Grafana, the Elastic stack, or equivalent), and a track record of setting observability and monitoring standards that translate into actionable insights for product teams downstream. You've also defined SLOs, run postmortems, and improved the on-call experiences you've personally experienced.

 - Comfortable owning systems end-to-end, from the IaC that provisions them to the runbooks that recover them.

 - Experience working in or alongside small, fas

notes: High growth phase with multiple sales and engineering roles
